title: artemisia vulgaris subsp. douglasiana
intro: Artemisia vulgaris subsp. douglasiana, also known as Douglas' mugwort, is a subspecies of the common mugwort plant, native to North America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties, which are attributed to its high content of artemisinin, a bioactive compound. The medicinal properties of Douglas' mugwort have been utilized to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and certain types of parasites. Herbal preparations of Douglas' mugwort include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote digestive health, alleviate symptoms of malaria, and support the immune system.
